history.content:
<thought>
The agent is facing north, but the cell directly ahead is lava. The left side is a wall, but the right side is a floor cell. No goal is visible in the observation. To explore, I should turn right to face east, then I can move forward into the safe floor cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is not visible in the current observation. The only safe adjacent cell is directly north (floor '.'), as west is lava and east is a wall. Moving north will reveal more of the map and potentially lead to the goal. Turning would result in facing a wall or lava, making forward movement impossible. Therefore, the best action is to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>I see a safe floor cell directly ahead. The goal is not visible in the current view, so I will move forward to explore further.</thought>
<action>MOVE_FORWARD</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north. The cell directly ahead is a floor ('.'), so it is safe to move forward. No goal is visible in the current observation. Moving forward will provide a new view and potentially get closer to the goal. The left neighbor is lava and the right neighbor is a wall, so forward is the only safe direction to move.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom center facing north. The cell directly ahead is a floor tile, which is safe. There are no visible goal tiles, so we need to explore further. Moving forward will reveal more of the map. There are walls and lava nearby, but the forward path is clear for now.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is lava, so I cannot move forward. The only safe adjacent cell I can see is to my west (left), which is a floor cell. I will turn left to face west, then I can move forward into that safe cell. The goal is not visible, so I need to explore the unknown areas.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is located at the top row, two columns to the right of my current column. There is a clear path directly forward (upwards) with empty cells all the way to row1 column4, then I can move right to the goal. The cell directly in front of me is an empty floor (.), so I can safely move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead (north) is a floor cell, safe to step on. The goal is located at the top row to the right. Moving forward along the clear path upward will bring me closer to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north. The cell directly ahead (5,3) is a floor cell. The goal G is located at (2,5), which is reachable by moving north four steps and then east two steps. Since the path ahead is clear of lava and walls, I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is located at (4,6) from my current position at (7,4) facing north. The cell directly ahead (6,4) is safe floor, and I can proceed north to eventually reach the goal by moving north twice, then turning east, and moving east twice. The immediate safe step is to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is located two cells north and two cells east from my position. The cell directly in front of me is a floor cell, so I can safely move forward. I will move forward to approach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
